title: Dog Ate Moldy Food: Mycotoxin Triage and Next Steps
meta_desc: If your dog ate moldy food, they may be at risk for mycotoxin poisoning. Learn about symptoms, urgency levels, and why a photo can save their life.
content_html:
TL;DR: Dogs eating moldy food can ingest dangerous tremorgenic mycotoxins that cause rapid neurological issues like tremors or seizures. Seek immediate veterinary care if your dog shows signs of shaking, stumbling, or excessive panting.

What happens if my dog eats moldy food from the trash?

When dogs consume moldy food from the trash, they are often ingesting tremorgenic mycotoxins. These are toxic compounds produced by certain types of fungi that can grow on bread, cheese, nuts, and decaying organic matter. Unlike a simple upset stomach, these toxins specifically target the nervous system and can cause rapid illness.

Is my dog eating moldy food a veterinary emergency?

  • The urgency level is Medium, but it can become High very quickly.
  • While some dogs may only show mild gastrointestinal distress, others can develop severe muscle tremors or seizures within minutes to hours of ingestion.
  • If your dog is stumbling, panting excessively, or shaking, it is a medical emergency that requires immediate veterinary intervention.

How can taking a photo of the mold help my veterinarian?

  • If you discovered your dog in the trash, take a quick photo of the moldy items before cleaning up.
  • Providing your veterinarian with a visual of the mold can help them assess the risk level and determine the best course of treatment.
  • Knowing the color of the mold and the type of food involved provides valuable context for the clinical team to prioritize care.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

In cases of suspected mycotoxicosis in dogs that have ingested moldy food, immediate corrective actions are crucial. The first step is to remove the source of the toxin by changing the feed, even if a specific mycotoxin is not identified. Thoroughly inspect storage bins, mixing equipment, and feeders for caking, molding, or musty odors, and clean and sanitize with hypochlorite (laundry bleach) to reduce contaminating fungi. Analyze the food source for known mycotoxins and consider spore counts or fungal cultures to assess potential mycotoxin production. There are no specific antidotes for mycotoxins, but the absorption of some mycotoxins (e.g., aflatoxin) may be prevented by aluminosilicates.

Chapter: Toxicology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 3005)

faqs.question: How long does it take for symptoms to show?
faqs.answer: Symptoms of mycotoxin poisoning usually appear within 30 minutes to 3 hours after ingestion.

faqs.question: What are the common signs of mold poisoning in dogs?
faqs.answer: Common signs include vomiting, agitation, tremors, high fever, and in severe cases, seizures.

faqs.question: Should I wait for symptoms to appear before calling a vet?
faqs.answer: No. Because these toxins act quickly, it is best to call a veterinarian or a pet poison control hotline as soon as you realize the moldy food was ingested to discuss decontamination.
